name: Featherweight v2
description: A 300-question multi-choice benchmark for the featherweight class of language models (sub-1B-parameter). Ten categories of thirty questions each: geography, history, arithmetic, science, language, vocabulary, common-sense, logic, categorization, reading. Answer positions balanced 75/75/75/75 across A/B/C/D. v2 is content-disjoint from v1 (no question is reused) and scales v1's per-category answer-position pattern by 3x so that v1 and v2 scores remain directly comparable. Released as the contamination-resistant successor to v1 per the v1 model card commitment.
intended_use: Comparing small language models on basic capabilities. A 200M-class model trained on a general corpus should score meaningfully above 25% chance with headroom for improvement. Use in place of v1 when there is any risk that a model under evaluation may have seen v1 in its training corpus. The 3x larger item count vs. v1 reduces per-question variance and gives more statistical power for per-category accuracy reporting.
scoring: 1 point per correct answer. Report accuracy and per-category breakdown.
